questions.q: The coordinates of the point in the xy-plane farthest from the origin is?
questions.explanation: The distance formula is √(x^2 + y^2) and maximum distance is obtained when both x and y are positive.

questions.q: Which of the following plane is parallel to the plane 2x + 3y - z = 0?
questions.explanation: For two planes to be parallel, their normal vectors must be proportional.

questions.q: The direction ratios of the line passing through the points (4, -1, 0) and (3, 1, 2) are?
questions.explanation: The direction ratios can be found by subtracting the coordinates of the two points.

questions.q: If the axes are rotated so that the new x-axis makes an angle of 60° with the old x-axis, then the direction ratios of the x-axis will be?
questions.explanation: The direction ratios can be found using trigonometric functions.

questions.q: The equation of the line passing through the point (1, 2, 3) and parallel to the vector 2i - 3j + 4k is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a line can be found using the point and the direction vector.

questions.q: The distance between the parallel planes x + y + z = λ and x + y + z = μ is?
questions.explanation: The distance between two parallel planes is the absolute value of the difference between the constants.

questions.q: If the foot of the perpendicular from the origin to the plane 2x + 3y - 4z = 0 is (α, β, γ), then the value of α + β + γ is?
questions.explanation: The foot of the perpendicular is the point where the perpendicular line intersects the plane.

questions.q: The equation of the plane passing through the points (1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6), and (7, 8, 9) is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a plane can be found using three non-collinear points.

questions.q: The distance of the point (2, 3, 4) from the plane x + y + z = 0 is?
questions.explanation: The distance formula is based on the perpendicular distance from a point to a plane.

questions.q: If the axes are rotated so that the new y-axis makes an angle of 45° with the old z-axis, then the direction ratios of the z-axis will be?
questions.explanation: The direction ratios can be found using trigonometric functions.

questions.q: The equation of the sphere with center (1, 2, 3) and radius 4 is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a sphere can be found using the center and the radius.

questions.q: The equation of the plane perpendicular to the line x = 2t, y = 3t, z = t and passing through the point (1, 2, 3) is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a plane can be found using the normal vector and a point on the plane.

questions.q: The distance between the parallel planes x + y + z = 0 and x + y + z = 1 is?
questions.explanation: The distance between two parallel planes is the absolute value of the difference between the constants.

questions.q: If the axes are rotated so that the new z-axis makes an angle of 30° with the old y-axis, then the direction ratios of the y-axis will be?
questions.explanation: The direction ratios can be found using trigonometric functions.

questions.q: The equation of the cylinder with axis x = 2y = z and radius 3 is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a cylinder can be found using the axis and the radius.

questions.q: The equation of the cone with axis x = 2y = z and vertex (1, 2, 3) is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a cone can be found using the axis and the vertex.

questions.q: The equation of the plane passing through the points (1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6), and (7, 8, 9) and parallel to the vector 2i + 3j - k is?
questions.explanation: The equation of a plane can be found using three non-collinear points and a normal vector.

questions.q: The distance of the point (2, 3, 4) from the plane x + y + z = 1 is?
questions.explanation: The distance formula is based on the perpendicular distance from a point to a plane.
